title: charset
text: charset Set default character set used by the Sybase open client. USYS$SYB_PARAMS = charset CharacterSetName | off Arguments CharacterSetName —name of a character set equivalent used by the Uniface application. This is the character set name and not the character set ID. off —disable character set conversion Description Use the charset option when you need to: Convert the client default character set in Sybase to a set which is equivalent to that used by Uniface. Use Unicode characters in the SQL Workbench, sql ProcScript statement, or error messages. In this case, specify unicode connector option and charset ut8 . Character Set Conversion Uniface and the Sybase Open Client Library (Client-Library) may use different character sets. When this happens, the connector must convert the client default character set in Sybase to a set which is equivalent to that used by Uniface. If you do not use this connector option, Uniface assumes that the Uniface character set matches the Sybase character set, or that the conversion is performed by Sybase. You can check whether character set conversion is performed with the select @@char_convert query. This query can be issued from within the Uniface SQL Workbench, or by the Sybase ISQL tool. If the query returns 0, no conversion is performed. If it returns 1, conversion is performed. You can check the client’s default character set with the select @@client_csname query. Possible return values depend on your installed character set. For example, ISO_1 and CP850. You can check the server’s default character set with the sp_helpsort stored procedure. Disable Character Set Conversion When the Sybase Client and Sybase SQL Server are configured for different character sets, Sybase SQL Server performs automatic character set conversion. In some situations, this conversion is not recommended. Therefore, character set conversion can be disabled with the following SYB connector option: USYS$SYB_PARAMS charset off Copy Example: Enabling Unicode Support usys$syb_params charset utf8, unicode, smp, keyword Copy Related Links unicode Enable or disable Unicode support in the SYB connector.
